l2_summary: The WHO has launched a program to deliver free cancer medicines to children in poor countries, starting with a pilot phase in countries like Mongolia. During this initial stage, the initiative expects to help approximately _____ (6) _____ children in 30 hospitals. While wealthy nations enjoy survival rates of about _____ (7) _____ percent, poorer countries see rates under 30 percent. Over the next five to seven years, the platform aims to expand to _____ (8) _____ countries. According to the WHO, around _____ (9) _____ children globally are diagnosed with cancer annually. Many die due to supply chain problems, incorrect treatments, or the use of _____ (10) _____ medicines. The program, which began in _____ (11) _____, is a partnership with St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital, which contributed _____ (12) _____ dollars for its creation. The WHO emphasizes that the medicine supply will be _____ (13) _____ and free of charge. Organizers are currently working on the program's long-term _____ (14) _____ to keep it running. Finally, governments are showing more interest because childhood cancer is becoming a leading _____ (15) _____ of death in wealthier nations.

4.A: It is extremely cold, but parts of the park remain open.

4.C: It forces the park to close completely to visitors.

l2_summary: Yellowstone, established in _____ (6) _____, is the world’s first national park, located mostly in _____ (7) _____. It sits on top of an active _____ (8) _____ which creates heat and pressure underground. This pressure results in more than _____ (9) _____ geysers, with "Old Faithful" erupting roughly every 90 minutes. Despite very cold winters, the park is always open to _____ (10) _____. One notable landscape is the canyon containing the _____ (11) _____ which drops 93 meters. After being damaged by _____ (12) _____ in 1988, the park has recovered with new plants and trees. Yellowstone is also home to the US's largest group of _____ (13) _____, including about _____ (14) _____ wild bison counted in the year _____ (15) _____.
